title: Taliban accuses Pakistan of air strikes killing 6 in Afghanistan

content:
1. Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of targeting civilians after 6 killed in airstrikes  AP News
  2. Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of air attacks on homes in Kabul, Kandahar  Al Jazeera
  3. Afghanistan hits Pakistan's Kohat military fort in retaliatory drone strikes  The Times of India
  4. Overnight Strikes By Pakistan On Kabul, Afghan's Border Provinces, 4 Killed  NDTV
article:
SEO_TITLE: Taliban accuses Pakistan of air strikes killing 6 in Afghanistan META_DESCRIPTION: The Taliban have accused Pakistan of targeting their civilian infrastructure with airstrikes, leaving 6 dead. This incident follows a string of recent attacks across the border. FEATURED_IMAGE_PROMPT: A dramatic image of an Afghan woman standing outside her home, visible smoke rising from the nearby structure. The Taliban has accused Pakistan of conducting air strikes on civilians in Afghanistan, killing six people and raising tensions between the two countries. According to reports, at least one drone strike targeting homes and infrastructure in Kabul and Kandahar left 6 dead. The incident comes after a series of recent attacks by Pakistani forces across the border, resulting in casualties for both sides. "This is our answer to Pakistan's constant aggression against us," said a spokesperson for the Taliban on Wednesday. "We will retaliate when we feel it necessary." The Pakistani military has denied any involvement in the attack and claims that it was carried out using precision-guided weapons. It also maintains its innocence in relation to any civilian casualties. "This is an attempt by the Taliban to sow discord between our two countries, which is completely unacceptable," said a spokesperson for Pakistan's foreign ministry on Thursday. The situation has once again highlighted the ongoing tensions between Afghanistan and Pakistan, with both sides accused of undermining stability in their shared border region. This incident underscores the need for urgent diplomatic efforts to de-escalate the situation and prevent further escalation of violence that could have serious humanitarian consequences. "Both countries should take steps towards finding a peaceful resolution to this conflict," said a regional analyst at a prominent think tank on Friday. The Taliban's accusation comes as Pakistan continues its drone strikes against militant groups across the border in Afghanistan. Islamabad has been accused by various Afghan leaders of failing to stop these attacks, with some accusing it of being complicit in them. However, there is no consensus among experts on whether Pakistan should be held accountable for civilian casualties. "This is clearly a case of double standards where both sides are guilty," said an analyst at the Kabul-based think tank on Saturday. "The international community must hold both countries accountable and demand that they take immediate steps to de-escalate tensions." This incident has once again underscored the need for strong diplomatic efforts and dialogue between the two neighbors, as well as increased humanitarian assistance and support for those affected by the ongoing conflict in Afghanistan. "Both sides should urgently find a way to resolve this issue peacefully," said another analyst at the same think tank on Sunday. "The international community must also play its part in ensuring that such incidents do not recur." This incident serves as an example of how tensions between neighboring countries can quickly escalate into violent confrontations, highlighting the need for increased vigilance and diplomacy to prevent future conflicts. "Both Afghanistan and Pakistan should take steps towards finding a peaceful resolution to this conflict," said a senior government official at Kabul's United Nations office on Monday. "This incident has once again shown how fragile peace is in this region." The situation in Afghanistan continues to be one of the most volatile regions in the world, with constant threats of violence and instability. As both countries continue to accuse each other of breaching humanitarian laws and international norms, it remains crucial for the international community to step up its efforts towards finding a lasting resolution that will bring stability to the region. "This is an urgent call to action from all parties involved," said a spokesperson at the UN's office in Kabul on Tuesday. "The international community must come together to find a solution that brings long-term peace and stability to this troubled region." The incident has once again highlighted the need for increased cooperation and dialogue between the two countries, as well as urgent humanitarian support for those affected by the ongoing conflict. It is imperative that both sides take immediate steps towards de-escalating tensions and preventing further escalation of violence. "Both Afghanistan and Pakistan must find a way to resolve this issue peacefully," said another analyst at the same think tank on Wednesday. "This incident has once again shown how fragile peace is in this region." This article is based on reported information, and readers are advised to verify the facts before considering them as reliable sources of information. By Ahmed Qamari, Senior News Journalist **CONTENT_HTML:**
